heading: Hazardous waste electronic manifest system
label: 6939g Hazardous waste electronic manifest system

children.heading: Board
children.label: 1 Board
children.text: The term “Board” means the Hazardous Waste Electronic Manifest System Advisory Board established under subsection (f).

children.heading: Fund
children.label: 2 Fund
children.text: The term “Fund” means the Hazardous Waste Electronic Manifest System Fund established by subsection (d).

children.heading: Person
children.label: 3 Person
children.text: The term “person” includes an individual, corporation (including a Government corporation), company, association, firm, partnership, society, joint stock company, trust, municipality, commission, Federal agency, State, political subdivision of a State, or interstate body.

children.heading: System
children.label: 4 System
children.text: The term “system” means the hazardous waste electronic manifest system established under subsection (b).

children.label: A
children.text: is required to use a manifest to comply with any Federal or State requirement to track the shipment, transportation, and receipt of hazardous waste or other material that is shipped from the site of generation to an off-site facility for treatment, storage, disposal, or recycling; and

children.label: i
children.text: elects to use the system to complete and transmit an electronic manifest format; or

children.label: ii
children.text: submits to the system for data processing purposes a paper copy of the manifest (or data from such a paper copy), in accordance with such regulations as the Administrator may promulgate to require such a submission.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: b Establishment
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , the Administrator shall establish a hazardous waste electronic manifest system that may be used by any user.October 5, 2012

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In accordance with paragraph (4), the Administrator may impose on users such reasonable service fees as the Administrator determines to be necessary to pay costs incurred in developing, operating, maintaining, and upgrading the system, including any costs incurred in collecting and processing data from any paper manifest submitted to the system after the date on which the system enters operation.

children.label: A
children.text: collect the fees described in paragraph (1) from the users in advance of, or as reimbursement for, the provision by the Administrator of system-related services; and

children.label: B
children.text: deposit the fees in the Fund.

children.label: I
children.text: materials and supplies;

children.label: II
children.text: contracting and consulting;

children.label: III
children.text: overhead;

children.label: IV
children.text: information technology (including costs of hardware, software, and related services);

children.label: V
children.text: information management;

children.label: VI
children.text: collection of service fees;

children.label: VII
children.text: reporting and accounting; and

children.label: VIII
children.text: project management; and

children.label: ii
children.text: costs of employment of direct and indirect Government personnel dedicated to establishing, managing, and maintaining the system.

children.label: I
children.text: result in the collection of an aggregate amount for deposit in the Fund that is sufficient and not more than reasonably necessary to cover current and projected system-related costs (including any necessary system upgrades); and

children.label: II
children.text: minimize, to the maximum extent practicable, the accumulation of unused amounts in the Fund.

children.heading: Exception for initial period of operation
children.label: ii Exception for initial period of operation
children.text: The requirement described in clause (i)(II) shall not apply to any additional fees that accumulate in the Fund, in an amount that does not exceed $2,000,000, during the 3-year period beginning on the date on which the system enters operation.

children.label: I
children.text: initially, at the time at which initial development costs of the system have been recovered by the Administrator such that the service fee may be reduced to reflect the elimination of the system development component of the fee; and

children.label: II
children.text: periodically thereafter, upon receipt and acceptance of the findings of any annual accounting or auditing report under subsection (d)(3), if the report discloses a significant disparity for a fiscal year between the funds collected from service fees under this subsection for the fiscal year and expenditures made for the fiscal year to provide system-related services.

children.heading: Crediting and availability of fees
children.label: 4 Crediting and availability of fees
children.text: Fees authorized under this section shall be collected and available for obligation only to the extent and in the amount provided in advance in appropriations Acts.

children.heading: Hazardous Waste Electronic Manifest System Fund
children.label: d Hazardous Waste Electronic Manifest System Fund

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a revolving fund, to be known as the “Hazardous Waste Electronic Manifest System Fund”, consisting of such amounts as are deposited in the Fund under subsection (c)(2)(B).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Only to the extent provided in advance in appropriations Acts, on request by the Administrator, the Secretary of the Treasury shall transfer from the Fund to the Administrator amounts appropriated to pay costs incurred in developing, operating, maintaining, and upgrading the system under subsection (c).

children.heading: Use of funds by Administrator
children.label: B Use of funds by Administrator
children.text: Fees collected by the Administrator and deposited in the Fund under this section shall be available to the Administrator subject to appropriations Acts for use in accordance with this section without fiscal year limitation.

children.heading: Oversight of funds
children.label: C Oversight of funds
children.text: The Administrator shall carry out all necessary measures to ensure that amounts in the Fund are used only to carry out the goals of establishing, operating, maintaining, upgrading, managing, supporting, and overseeing the system.

children.label: I
children.text: the Chief Financial Officers Act of 1990 (; ) and amendments made by that Act; andPublic Law 101–576104 Stat. 2838

children.label: II
children.text: the Government Management Reform Act of 1994 (; ) and amendments made by that Act; andPublic Law 103–356108 Stat. 3410

children.label: ii
children.text: an accounting describing actual expenditures from the Fund for the period covered by the report for costs described in subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For the purpose of , the Fund shall be considered a component of an Executive agency.section 3515(c) of title 31

children.label: I
children.text: the fees collected and disbursed under this section;

children.label: II
children.text: the reasonableness of the fee structure in place as of the date of the audit to meet current and projected costs of the system;

children.label: III
children.text: the level of use of the system by users; and

children.label: IV
children.text: the success to date of the system in operating on a self-sustaining basis and improving the efficiency of tracking waste shipments and transmitting waste shipment data.

children.label: I
children.text: conduct the annual audit described in clause (ii); and

children.label: II
children.text: submit to the Administrator a report that describes the findings and recommendations of the Inspector General resulting from the audit.

children.heading: Authority to enter into contracts funded by service fees
children.label: 1 Authority to enter into contracts funded by service fees
children.text: After consultation with the Secretary of Transportation, the Administrator may enter into 1 or more information technology contracts with entities determined to be appropriate by the Administrator (referred to in this subsection as “contractors”) for the provision of system-related services.

children.heading: Term of contract
children.label: 2 Term of contract
children.text: A contract awarded under this subsection shall have a term of not more than 10 years.

children.label: A
children.text: is performance-based;

children.label: B
children.text: identifies objective outcomes; and

children.label: i
children.text: meets the needs of the user community (including States that rely on data contained in manifests);

children.label: ii
children.text: attracts sufficient user participation and service fee revenues to ensure the viability of the system;

children.label: iii
children.text: decreases the administrative burden on the user community; and

children.label: iv
children.text: provides the waste receipt data applicable to the biennial reports required by .section 6922(a)(6) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the service fee structure of the contractor that will form the basis for payments to the contractor; and

children.label: B
children.text: the fixed-share ratio of monthly service fee revenues from which the Administrator shall reimburse the contractor for system-related development, operation, and maintenance costs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the Administrator determines that sufficient funds are not made available for the continuation in a subsequent fiscal year of a contract entered into under this subsection, the Administrator may cancel or terminate the contract.

children.heading: Negotiation of amounts
children.label: B Negotiation of amounts
children.text: The amount payable in the event of cancellation or termination of a contract entered into under this subsection shall be negotiated with the contractor at the time at which the contract is awarded.

children.heading: No effect on ownership
children.label: 6 No effect on ownership
children.text: Regardless of whether the Administrator enters into a contract under this subsection, the system shall be owned by the Federal Government.

children.heading: Hazardous Waste Electronic Manifest System Advisory Board
children.label: f Hazardous Waste Electronic Manifest System Advisory Board

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , the Administrator shall establish a board to be known as the “Hazardous Waste Electronic Manifest System Advisory Board”.October 5, 2012

children.label: A
children.text: 1 member shall be the Administrator (or a designee), who shall serve as Chairperson of the Board; and

children.label: i
children.text: at least 2 of whom shall have expertise in information technology;

children.label: ii
children.text: at least 3 of whom shall have experience in using or represent users of the manifest system to track the transportation of hazardous waste under this subchapter (or an equivalent State program); and

children.label: iii
children.text: at least 3 of whom shall be a State representative responsible for processing those manifests.

children.heading: Duties
children.label: 3 Duties
children.text: The Board shall meet annually to discuss, evaluate the effectiveness of, and provide recommendations to the Administrator relating to, the system.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , after consultation with the Secretary of Transportation, the Administrator shall promulgate regulations to carry out this section.October 5, 2012

children.heading: Inclusions
children.label: B Inclusions
children.text: The regulations promulgated pursuant to subparagraph (A) may include such requirements as the Administrator determines to be necessary to facilitate the transition from the use of paper manifests to the use of electronic manifests, or to accommodate the processing of data from paper manifests in the electronic manifest system, including a requirement that users of paper manifests submit to the system copies of the paper manifests for data processing purposes.

children.label: I
children.text: the person that certifies that the information provided in the manifest is accurately described; and

children.label: II
children.text: the person that acknowledges receipt of the manifest;

children.label: ii
children.text: if the manifest is electronically submitted, State authority to access paper printout copies of the manifest from the system; and

children.label: iii
children.text: access to all publicly available information contained in the manifest.

children.heading: Effective date of regulations
children.label: 2 Effective date of regulations
children.text: Any regulation promulgated by the Administrator under paragraph (1) and in accordance with relating to electronic manifesting of hazardous waste shall take effect in each State as of the effective date specified in the regulation.section 6923 of this title

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 3 Administration
children.text: The Administrator shall carry out regulations promulgated under this subsection in each State unless the State program is fully authorized to carry out such regulations in lieu of the Administrator.

children.heading: Requirement of compliance with respect to certain States
children.label: h Requirement of compliance with respect to certain States

children.text: complete the facility portion of the applicable manifest;
sign and date the facility certification; and
submit to the system a final copy of the manifest for data processing purposes.

children.heading: Authorization for start-up activities
children.label: i Authorization for start-up activities
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated $2,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2013 through 2015 for start-up activities to carry out this section, to be offset by collection of user fees under subsection (c) such that all such appropriated funds are offset by fees as provided in subsection (c).
